data_gaps: route_participation and weighted opportunity (red-zone/end-zone usage) not retrievable -- needs nflverse participation/pbp, not pulled
injury status unavailable in frozen world
rookies with no NFL usage data (confidence capped): Jeanty, Hampton, Harvey, K.Johnson, Judkins, Henderson, Blue, Skattebo, Tuten
J.K. Dobbins absent from DEN depth chart (captured 2025-04-27, pre-signing)
Cam Skattebo absent from NYG depth chart (captured 2025-04-26)

rankings.rationale: 57.9% carry share rising to 60.7% late, 73.4% snaps, in the league's most run-committed offense (41.9% pass rate, 62.9 plays/game). Largest secure carry pie in the pool; efficiency regressed to role-average still clears 20 PPG.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: PHI Week 1-3 pass rate above 50%, or carry share below 50% in a healthy stretch.

rankings.rationale: 61.4% carry share plus 14.1% target share at 75.4% snaps, late-window carry share 65.5%, and zero vacated-volume ambiguity in Atlanta. The best combined rush+receiving volume in the pool needs no efficiency assumptions.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Allgeier above 30% of carries in Weeks 1-2, or snap share under 65%.

rankings.rationale: Last-8 carry share 50.4% (up from 46.8%) at 25.0 PPG in the pool's highest-volume offense (63.8 plays/game) -- the late window rules. Montgomery's stable RB2 role caps the share ceiling, and his 55.3% snap share means efficiency is carrying scoring I partially regress.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Montgomery absent (up), or snap share under 55% through Week 3 (down).

rankings.rationale: Pool-best 73.7% carry share (75.9% late) at 80.3% snaps with no listed competition -- the most monopolized backfield in the data. The 7.5% target share caps the PPR ceiling, keeping him under the dual-threat tier.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share above 10% early (up a tier); carry share below 60% (down).

rankings.rationale: In full-PPR I weight his 87 targets (15.3% share) as the most bankable volume in this tier, and his carry share ROSE to 53.9% late while Mostert's departure vacated 88 carries. Both trend lines up; second pass moves him ahead of the pure rushers.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share below 11% over Weeks 1-3, or a 40%+ committee partner.

rankings.rationale: 325 carries at 58.7% share in a 46.3%-pass offense -- elite, stable rush volume. The season-vs-late-8 PPG gap (19.8 vs 16.9) on flat share is the efficiency regression my rules mandate, and the 5.3% target share offers no PPR cushion.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A 10+ carry/game committee partner emerging in Baltimore.

rankings.rationale: 83.0% carry share and 85.8% snap share over the final 8 games is near-total backfield control, worth 20.6 PPG in that window; on this pass I weight that monopoly more heavily than run 1 did. Cincinnati's 63.2% pass rate is the structural cap.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Moss or a newcomer at 30%+ of early carries.

rankings.rationale: 57.2% carry share on 301 carries, rising late (58.1%, 20.4 PPG), in a 47.7%-pass offense returning its full touch distribution. Zero vacated-volume ambiguity; the projection is pure volume continuity.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Green Bay rotation pushing his share below 50% in the first month.

rankings.rationale: 86.4% snap share and 73.7% carry share on 316 carries -- volume security a tier above his scoring, which the thin 7.9% target share and soft late window (15.3 PPG) explain. Ranked on the snap monopoly; Kupp's 214 vacated targets don't reroute to backs at this usage.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Corum above 25% of carries early, or snap share below 75%.

rankings.rationale: Elite 4-game shares (54.3% carries, 17.9% targets, 72.3% snaps) and 209 vacated carries from Mason's exit leave him the unchallenged SF feature back -- but 4 games after an injury-lost year, with injury status structurally unavailable in my data, is a stated blind spot. Projected on a 14-game season.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Any camp absence (down 5+); healthy 65%+ snap share Weeks 1-2 (up toward top 5).

rankings.rationale: Carry share 42.9%→48.3% late with PPG rising to 18.3 in a 62-plays/game offense that returns everything -- the cleanest ascending-role trend in the pool, weighted per my late-window rule. White's fading share is the confirming signal.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: White out-snapping Irving in Weeks 1-2.

rankings.rationale: 21.3% target share (89 targets), stable late at 21.6% -- the pool's best PPR floor -- plus 58.2% of carries. Late-window PPG (16.1) under season (18.95) points to efficiency cooling on stable volume, so I project near the late window.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Target share below 15% Weeks 1-3, or a 40%+ committee.

rankings.rationale: Rookie (confidence capped by rule) with the pool's cleanest inherited-volume path: 233 vacated carries and an immediate RB1 listing over Mostert. Structure, not usage data, sets this rank.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Committee signals with Mostert/White, or Week 1-2 carry share under 50%.

rankings.rationale: 61.1% carry share, 14.1% target share, 72.4% snaps -- top-10 volume in 2024 -- with mild late-window erosion and the pool's second-lowest team play volume. On this pass I discount the Feb-captured chart less: no volume-taker was added per any of my sources.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Allen above 35% of early snaps.

rankings.rationale: Late-window bell-cow usage (72.4% carries, 85.6% snaps) argues top-12; Dowdle's arrival as listed RB2 in a 56.3-plays/game offense argues a real split. I project the majority side of a new committee.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Dowdle above 35% of carries in the first month.

rankings.rationale: 66.9% carry share in both windows with a 12.2% target share -- volume held rock-steady even as late-window scoring (14.0) cooled from season (17.2), the classic efficiency-not-opportunity decline I project through. Chubb behind him is insurance, not a proven share threat.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Chubb at 30%+ of carries in any healthy September week.

rankings.rationale: Late window BEAT season (17.6 vs 15.9 PPG) on a rising 13.5% target share -- growing receiving volume is the most PPR-valuable trend in this tier. Benson's 19.1% share never threatened the lead role.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Benson above 30% of September carries.

rankings.rationale: 62.5% carry share and 13.7% target share in 11 games is top-10 per-game volume; the 11-game availability and Charbonnet's proven 40%+ relief share are why it doesn't rank there. Second pass weighs the availability risk slightly more than run 1.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Charbonnet above 40% of snaps with Walker healthy.

rankings.rationale: 45.2% carry share, 47.7% snaps, both LOWER late (39.8%/40.9%) -- committee usage whose 16.7 PPG rode touchdown efficiency my rules regress. The three-back rotation behind him is structural, not incidental.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Week 1-3 snap share above 60% (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped confidence) atop a backfield with 296 vacated carries, but sharing it with a 263-carry-durable veteran makes a committee the base case. Best structure after Jeanty; discounted for the split.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Harris opening at 45%+ of carries (down 5+); 60%+ early share (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: The most stable committee share in the data -- 42.2% season, 41.1% late -- inside the pool's highest-volume offense. Scoring leans on high-value usage my tools can't see (red-zone weighting is a listed gap), so I hold the projection just under his 15.8 PPG output.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Detroit moving past a 60/40 split toward Gibbs.

rankings.rationale: Secure volume (58.7% carries, 66.8% snaps, no listed threat) with poor output (12.6 PPG) -- my methodology projects role-average efficiency onto the volume, which lands slightly above his 2024 line. New-coordinator play volume is the stated unknown.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A new back taking 30%+ of first-team camp carries.

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) inheriting the pool's largest vacated rush volume (376 carries) as listed RB1 in a 48.4%-pass offense. Warren's locked passing-down role bounds this to a two-down majority.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Warren opening as carry leader (down 5+); 60%+ share by Week 3 (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 61.9% carry share on 260 carries is top-12 volume trapped in a bottom-tier environment (57.7 plays/game, 10.4 late PPG). Volume is the signal, environment the cap; Spears' stable 29% bounds the downside.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Spears above 40%, or team play volume above 62/game (up).

rankings.rationale: Balanced 55.8%/12.2% carry/target shares over 17 games, but Minnesota traded real assets for Mason and vacated 137 carries -- revealed intent to cut the workload. I project a low-50s share with receiving work intact; slightly more committee-worried than run 1.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Mason above 40% of September carries.

rankings.rationale: 7-game injury year at 47.2% share makes 2024 nearly uninformative; the RB1 listing with Hunt behind, KC's 61.8 plays/game, and 163 vacated targets frame a rebound whose size my data can't verify. Sample-size risk stated.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 55%+ carry share Weeks 1-2 (up 5+); even split with Hunt (down).

rankings.rationale: Rising trend: 45.3%→55.4% carry share, 66.8% late snaps, listed RB1 with 86 vacated carries. The drafted rookie absent from my chart data is the acknowledged risk to the trend case.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Skattebo or Singletary above 35% of early carries.

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) listed RB1 with 139 vacated carries and 146 vacated targets -- but Dobbins' post-capture signing is invisible to my tools, so the split is unverifiable. Lead-committee projection with the gap stated.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Dobbins taking 40%+ of Week-1 work (down 5+); clean RB1 September (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) behind Ford on the chart, but Cleveland's 229 vacated carries and 258 vacated targets are the pool's largest combined redistribution in a 61.9-plays/game offense. Structure strong, chart order the discount.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: RB1 usage by Week 1 (up 5+); three-way rotation (down).

rankings.rationale: 53.1% share slipping to 48.8% late, in a 57.4-plays/game offense that just drafted his replacement-candidate (Henderson RB2). Narrowing-majority projection; the trend and the draft signal both point down.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Henderson held to change-of-pace usage through September (up); an even split (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: This rank is an allocation of Dallas' 369 vacated carries to the listed RB1, made against his own collapsed 17.2% late-2024 share -- structure over personal trend, with Blue the obvious claimant if he falters.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Blue at 40%+ of early carries (down 5+); 55%+ September share (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) listed RB2 with minimal inherited volume (45 vacated carries) -- he must take share from an incumbent rather than inherit it. Draft capital as revealed intent is the upside argument my data can only gesture at.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Passing-down work plus 40%+ carries by October (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 50.9% share and 14.75 PPG over 13 games is real recent volume, and Denver's 139 vacated carries fit him -- but he's absent from every chart in my data (DEN captured pre-signing), so the role is a stated gap. Third pass puts him a notch above run 1 on the strength of the 2024 usage itself.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Confirmed lead-back usage Week 1 (up 5+); clear backup role (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: 263 durable carries at 49.3% share, now RB2 behind a rookie with 296 vacated carries to split -- the pie is big enough that even the minority share sustains flex value. Durability is his one elite metric.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Hampton at 65%+ of carries by October (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: The fading half of Tampa's split (34.5%→31.4% carries) with a preserved 11.3% target share -- PPR passing-down value in a 62-plays/game offense, declining trend priced in.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: An Irving absence (up 8+); losing passing downs too (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Locked passing-down role (13.7% late target share) plus a share of 376 vacated carries while a rookie settles -- the floor is stable and the early-season upside real.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Warren opening as actual carry leader (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: Listed RB1 with committee usage (42.6% carries, 53.9% snaps) in the pool's lowest-volume offense, ahead of two listed challengers under a new staff (play volume unknowable -- stated blind spot).
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 55%+ early share under the new staff (up 5+); split first-team work (down).

rankings.rationale: 40.7% late-window carry share in relief plus 17 games of durability -- the most proven contingency profile in the pool given Walker's 11-game season. Slightly ahead of the standalone RB2s on this pass because the contingency is likelier than most.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Walker injury or announced committee (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: Two-down RB1 at 41.7% share with sagging late output (8.7 PPG) and no passing-down role (Ekeler's) -- the same split continuing in a good-volume offense.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Share above 55% (up); a new early-down back (down).

rankings.rationale: 12.2% target share (12.9% late) at only 22.1% of carries -- a pure PPR passing-down profile with 118 vacated team targets as mild upside; 12 games played is the availability caveat.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Passing-down role shifting elsewhere (down 5+).

rankings.rationale: Stable 29-31% carry share with dual usage behind a durable starter in a slow offense -- one injury from RB2 value, capped until then.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Pollard absence or 40%+ share week (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: 45.0% 2024 share (fading to 29.4% late) traded into a Minnesota backfield with 137 vacated carries -- the trade is revealed intent, the late fade the counter-signal. Situation-change discount applied.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: 40%+ of Minnesota's September carries (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: Sole listed backup to a 4-game-2024 starter after Mason's 209 vacated carries consolidated the role -- the pool's highest-leverage handcuff, moved up one pass on the contingency odds.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Any McCaffrey absence (up 10+); a veteran signing (down).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) RB2 behind a weak incumbent with 369 vacated carries in the room -- the most volatile backfield in the pool, and he is the cheap side of it.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Leading the backfield in touches any September week (up 8+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie absent from my chart data entirely (NYG captured 2025-04-26) -- a double data gap ranked on structural inference behind an ascending starter and a veteran RB2.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Preseason usage ahead of Singletary (up 5+).

rankings.rationale: 19.1% share with no growth trend behind a 236-carry starter -- pure handcuff value on a stable ladder.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Conner absence (up 10+).

rankings.rationale: Third slice of a three-way committee (23.0% carries, 4.2% targets) -- droppable depth whose value is entirely the promotion scenario.

evidence.value: Cook RB1, Johnson RB2, Davis RB3 (BUF)

rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: A Cook absence elevating him to 40%+ share (up 10+).

rankings.rationale: Rookie (capped) listed RB3 with two incumbents ahead in the pool's lowest-volume offense and little inherited volume (64 vacated carries) -- the weakest opportunity structure among pool rookies.
rankings.what_would_change_my_mind: Passing Bigsby on the usage ladder in September (up 5+).
